summary: Validate initial M3 H-003 and H-005 evidence
acceptance_criteria: Initial H-003 evidence records that at least three postwar governance methods have different 24-month outcomes and are not trivially dominated by direct annexation in the M3 fixture.

Initial H-005 evidence records whether character/vassal proposals or reason summaries are useful but not always optimal, using measurable scenario outcomes, visible reason-code coverage, and residual limitations.
Validation commands include real exit codes, key metrics, fixture seed/manifest details, and explicit limits; no 'should be better' or anecdotal-only claim is accepted.
Client/UI evidence covers appointment, bulk action, and reason display flows needed to inspect H-005; simulation evidence covers the 24-month H-003 comparison.
Any inconclusive or partial result is recorded as residual risk or BLOCKED/PARTIAL handoff instead of being inflated into M3 acceptance.
No feature implementation, broad optimization, formal historical content, Electron security surface, new production dependency, or roadmap/gate edit is hidden inside validation.
required_tests: Scenario or runner command compares three M3 governance arrangements over 24 months with recorded outcome metrics.
Playwright or Storybook/browser smoke covers appointment, bulk action, per-item rejection, and reason display flows.
node -e JSON parse/shape check for M3-H003-H005-VALIDATION-001

gameplay_designer.responsibility: H-003/H-005 validation design and outcome interpretation

notes: This is validation-focused and should not implement missing M3 systems except for tiny test harness adjustments needed to run accepted flows.
H-003/H-005 are initial M3 evidence, not final M5 playtest proof.
If the validation shows direct annexation dominates or reasons are not useful, route findings to gameplay_designer and systems_architect rather than adjusting product scope silently.
